info.name: RBAC – Owner (PUT /api/v1/rbac/owner/assign + transfer routes)
info.description:
E2E tests for the owner domain: PUT /assign, POST /transfer, PUT /transfer (accept), DELETE /transfer.

Folder execution order (orchestrated by make e2e-rbac-owner):
  1.  setup                    — register two users (owner + +xfrtgt target), verify both via Gmail OTP, login both
  2.  auth-guard               — 401 without Authorization header (JWT guard; assign / initiate / cancel)
  3.  validation-assign        — 422/400/413 input errors for PUT /assign
  4.  secret-guard             — 403 wrong secret for PUT /assign
  5.  assign-happy-path        — 201 PUT /assign (User A becomes owner)
  6.  owner-already-exists     — 409 repeat PUT /assign
  7.  rbac-guard               — 403 POST /transfer + DELETE /transfer when caller is not owner
  8.  validation-transfer      — 422/400 input errors for POST /transfer
  9.  transfer-errors          — 404 unknown target + 409 self-transfer
  10. transfer-initiate        — 201 POST /transfer (User A → User B, captures transfer_id)
  11. transfer-already-pending — 409 second POST /transfer while one is active
  12. validation-accept        — 422/400 input errors for PUT /rbac/owner/transfer (accept)
  13. accept-invalid           — 410 PUT /rbac/owner/transfer (accept) with bogus token (pending still alive)
  14. transfer-cancel          — 204 DELETE /transfer
  15. cancel-not-found         — 404 DELETE /transfer (nothing pending)
  16. rate-limiting-assign     — asgn:ip: burst=3 → 4th → 429 (Retry-After: 300 s)
  17. rate-limiting-transfer   — xfr:usr: 1 slot left after main flow → 2nd → 429 (Retry-After: 28800 s)
  18. rate-limiting-accept     — xfra:ip: burst=10 → 11th → 429 (Retry-After: 360 s)
  19. rate-limiting-cancel     — xfrc:usr: 8 slots left after main flow → 9th → 429 (Retry-After: 360 s)

Routes (from routes.go):
  POST   /api/v1/rbac/owner/assign           — secret-gated initial owner assignment (JWT required)
  POST   /api/v1/rbac/owner/transfer         — initiate ownership transfer (JWT + owner role)
  POST   /api/v1/rbac/owner/transfer  — accept transfer (no JWT; raw token is credential)
  DELETE /api/v1/rbac/owner/transfer         — cancel pending transfer (JWT + owner role)

Rate limits:
  asgn:ip:  — burst=3, rate=3/15 min per IP      → Retry-After: 300 s   (JWT-gated)
  xfr:usr:  — burst=3, rate=3/24 h per user      → Retry-After: 28800 s (JWT-gated)
  xfra:ip:  — burst=10, rate=10/1 h per IP       → Retry-After: 360 s   (no JWT)
  xfrc:usr: — burst=10, rate=10/1 h per user     → Retry-After: 360 s   (JWT-gated)

Rate-limit accounting (all run in ONE newman invocation; Redis flushed before the run):
  xfr:usr: consumed before rate-limiting-transfer: transfer-initiate(1) + transfer-already-pending(1) = 2; 1 remaining
  xfrc:usr: consumed before rate-limiting-cancel:  transfer-cancel(1)   + cancel-not-found(1)          = 2; 8 remaining
  asgn:ip: and xfra:ip: use unique X-Forwarded-For IPs for all non-RL folders (collection prerequest);
  the 127.0.0.1 bucket is untouched until the matching RL folder overrides _xff.

Environment variables required:
  bootstrap_secret   — must match BOOTSTRAP_SECRET in .env
  gmail_test_email   — Gmail address (owner user A); +xfrtgt alias is derived for User B
  gmail_access_token — OAuth2 token for Gmail API reads
  app_name           — matches APP_NAME in .env

Notes on what is NOT covered here (covered by internal tests):
  - accept happy path with real token  — raw token only arrives in User B's email; covered by store_test.go + service_test.go
  - user_not_found for assign          — a valid JWT cannot be issued for a non-existent user
  - user_not_active / not_verified for assign / transfer target — cannot create these states black-box and log in
  - DB failures → 500                  — service_test.go
  - All error responses carry code + JSON CT — handler_test.go

exec: // Increment request sequence counter and derive a unique X-Forwarded-For IP for every
// request so non-RL folders never exhaust any IP-based rate-limit bucket.
// Rate-limiting folders override _xff to 127.0.0.1 via their own folder prerequest.

item.name: setup
item.description: Registers User A (gmail_test_email → owner candidate) and User B (gmail_test_email+xfrtgt → transfer target), fetches OTPs from Gmail, verifies both accounts, logs both in, and captures tokens + IDs.

exec: if (!email) { console.error('[ERROR] gmail_test_email not set in environment.'); }
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for User A verification email...');
const q = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + ownerEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching email for User A.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode User A email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in User A email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] User A OTP stored:', match[1]);
if (!code) { console.error('[ERROR] own_code not set — User A Gmail fetch did not complete.'); }
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping User B Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for User B verification email...');
const q = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + targetEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired.'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching email for User B.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode User B email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in User B email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] User B OTP stored:', match[1]);
if (!code) { console.error('[ERROR] own_target_code not set — User B Gmail fetch did not complete.'); }

item.name: auth-guard
item.description: JWT middleware rejects requests without an Authorization header before any body or service logic is reached. Tests all three JWT-gated routes. Accept (/rbac/owner/transfer) is excluded — it has no JWT guard.

item.name: validation-assign
item.description: Body decode and secret validation for POST /assign. All requests carry a valid JWT. Fires after auth but before the secret comparison and service call.

exec: pm.request.body.update({ mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ secret: 'any', _pad: pad }) });

item.name: secret-guard
item.description: Constant-time secret comparison. Valid JWT, well-formed body, wrong secret → 403. Fires before service or DB call.

exec: pm.test('no user_id or role_name in body', () => {

item.name: assign-happy-path
item.description: Valid JWT + correct secret → 201. User A becomes the owner. Consumes the one-time slot.

item.name: owner-already-exists
item.description: Single-owner invariant: CountActiveOwners > 0 → 409 before any user lookup. Requires valid JWT + correct secret to reach the service layer.

exec: pm.test('no user_id or role_name in body', () => {

item.name: repeated call → 409 (owner guard fires before secret comparison)
rbac-guard
item.description: User B is not the owner — POST /transfer and DELETE /transfer must return 403. Uses own_target_access_token.

item.name: validation-transfer
item.description: Body decode and target_user_id validation for POST /transfer. All requests use the owner JWT. Fires after auth + RBAC guard but before service.

item.name: transfer-errors
item.description: Service-layer errors for POST /transfer: unknown target UUID → 404, self-transfer → 409.

item.name: transfer-initiate
item.description: Happy path for POST /transfer. User A (owner) initiates transfer to User B. Captures transfer_id. xfr:usr: count: 1.

item.name: transfer-already-pending
item.description: HasPendingTransferToken returns true while a transfer is active → 409. xfr:usr: count: 2.

item.name: validation-accept
item.description: Body decode and token field validation for PUT /rbac/owner/transfer (accept). No JWT required.

item.name: accept-invalid
item.description: Pending transfer exists but token does not match the stored bcrypt hash → 410 token_invalid. Token remains unconsumed.

exec: // bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword fails for the bogus token → ErrTransferTokenInvalid → 410.
// The token is NOT consumed — pending transfer remains active for transfer-cancel.

item.name: transfer-cancel
item.description: Owner cancels the pending transfer → 204 No Content. xfrc:usr: count: 1.

item.name: cancel-not-found
item.description: No pending transfer exists (was just cancelled) → 404 no_pending_transfer. xfrc:usr: count: 2.

item.name: rate-limiting-assign
item.description: asgn:ip: limiter exhaustion (burst=3, rate=3/900s per IP → Retry-After=300s). Folder prerequest pins _xff to 127.0.0.1. 3 warmups (→ 409 owner_already_exists each). 4th → 429.

item.name: warmup 3/3 — consume asgn:ip: last slot (→ not 429)
rate-limiting-transfer
item.description: xfr:usr: limiter exhaustion (burst=3, rate=3/86400s per user → Retry-After=28800s). 2 slots consumed by transfer-initiate + transfer-already-pending. 1 remaining warmup (→ 201, creates a pending transfer), then 2nd → 429.

item.name: warmup 1/1 — consume last xfr:usr: slot (→ 201 new pending transfer)

exec: // This is the 3rd POST /transfer for this user (burst=3). Creates a pending transfer.
// The token will be left pending until _e2e-db-clean runs.

item.name: rate-limiting-accept
item.description: xfra:ip: limiter exhaustion (burst=10, rate=10/3600s per IP → Retry-After=360s). Folder prerequest pins _xff to 127.0.0.1. A pending transfer exists from RL-transfer warmup; bogus token → 410 on each warmup. 11th → 429.

exec: pm.test('not rate-limited yet (xfra:ip: slot 10 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));

item.name: rate-limiting-cancel
item.description: xfrc:usr: limiter exhaustion (burst=10, rate=10/3600s per user → Retry-After=360s). 2 slots consumed by transfer-cancel + cancel-not-found. 8 remaining. Warmup 1 cancels the pending transfer from RL-transfer (→ 204); warmups 2-8 → 404; 9th → 429.

item.name: warmup 1/8 — consume xfrc:usr: slot (→ 204 cancels RL pending)

exec: pm.test('not rate-limited yet (xfrc:usr: slot 10 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));

variable.description: Auto-incremented counter for generating unique X-Forwarded-For IPs.
Current X-Forwarded-For value. Unique IP set by collection prerequest; overridden to 127.0.0.1 by IP-based RL folder prerequest.
Set by setup from gmail_test_email. User A (owner candidate).
6-digit OTP for User A fetched from Gmail during setup/register.
JWT access_token for User A. Required by all JWT-gated routes.
user_id (UUID) for User A, captured from setup/GET /me.
gmail_test_email+xfrtgt. Set by setup/register User B prerequest.
6-digit OTP for User B fetched from Gmail during setup/register User B.
JWT access_token for User B. Used in rbac-guard tests.
user_id (UUID) for User B, captured from setup/GET /me User B.
transfer_id UUID from POST /transfer response in transfer-initiate folder.
